# karma


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

This file will become your README and also the index of your
documentation.

## Developer Guide

If you are new to using `nbdev` here are some useful pointers to get you
started.

### Install karma in Development mode

``` sh
# make sure karma package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to karma
$ nbdev_prepare
```

## Usage

### Installation

Install latest from the GitHub
[repository](https://github.com/vedicreader/karma):

``` sh
$ pip install git+https://github.com/vedicreader/karma.git
```

or from [conda](https://anaconda.org/vedicreader/karma)

``` sh
$ conda install -c vedicreader karma
```

or from [pypi](https://pypi.org/project/karma/)

``` sh
$ pip install karma
```

### Documentation

Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub
[repository](https://github.com/vedicreader/karma)’s
[pages](https://vedicreader.github.io/karma/). Additionally you can find
package manager specific guidelines on
[conda](https://anaconda.org/vedicreader/karma) and
[pypi](https://pypi.org/project/karma/) respectively.
